invariant_definition: Action performed without attachment to its fruits — the central teaching of the *Bhagavad-Gītā*'s *karma-yoga* chapters (especially 2–4, 18). All Vedāntic schools accept *niṣkāma-karma* as the right form of action; they differ on its place: Advaita (preparation for *jñāna* via *citta-śuddhi*); Viśiṣṭādvaita (one aspect of integral *bhakti-yoga*); Dvaita (continuous mode of devotion to Viṣṇu). The user's position-brief (T8) makes *niṣkāma karma* structurally central — it is the action-grammar of the *sākṣi-caitanya* mode and connects to *līlā*, wu-wei, and the disinterestedness of Kant and Abhinavagupta's *rasa*.

per_school.definition: *Niṣkāma-karma* is *cittasuddhi*-yielding; it does not itself produce *mokṣa*. The Gītā's *karma-yoga* is read as *cittasuddhi-upāya*, parallel-but-not-equal to *jñāna*.

*Niṣkāma-karma* offered as *bhagavad-ārādhana* is itself an aspect of *bhakti-yoga*. The opposition Advaita draws between *karma* and *jñāna* is rejected; *karma* under *bhakti* continues for the realized.

translator_note: 'Desireless action' or 'action without attachment to fruits' both acceptable. Note: *niṣkāma* does not mean 'without motivation' — the action has a motive (the prescribed duty, devotion to the Lord), but lacks attachment to *its specific outcome for the agent*.
